results.possibleIdentity: Lawrence "Larry" Klein (1928–2013) was a prominent technical editor and director in the field of high-fidelity (Hi-Fi) audio and consumer electronics. He is best known for his long tenure at the magazine HiFi/Stereo Review (later renamed Stereo Review), where he served as Technical Editor in the late 1960s and eventually became Technical Director. Based in New York, Klein was a leading authority on audio technology during the formative years of the consumer electronics industry, authoring columns such as "Audio Questions and Answers" and "Audio News." He worked closely with the Hirsch-Houck Laboratories to provide technical evaluations of audio equipment and was a frequent panelist at major industry events, such as the New York High Fidelity Music Show. In the early 1960s, he produced the well-regarded reference recording "Art of the Koto" for Elektra Records. Given his role as a central technical figure in the New York electronics scene in the late 1960s and 1970s, he is a likely candidate for association with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that sought to pair technical experts with artists, although some E.A.T. records may also refer to a contemporary designer of the same name.
